title: arisarum simorrhinum var. subexertum
intro: Arisarum simorrhinum var. subexertum is a variety of the Arisarum simorrhinum plant, commonly known as the leopard's bane or arum, which is a type of flowering plant native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Arisarum simorrhinum var. subexertum include reducing inflammation and pain, treating skin conditions and wounds, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or ingested as a treatment for various health conditions.
